title: OnePlus ceases operations in the United States and Europe and confirms its exit from the Western market
summary: The smartphone brand, owned by Oppo, abandons the US and European markets after years of growth and decline.

title: Thinking Machines Lab, founded by OpenAI's former CTO, presents its first open model Inkling
summary: The 975 billion parameter model surpasses US labs but lags behind Chinese advances, according to the company.

title: AMD Ryzen 7 7700X3D reviews reveal lower performance than the 7800X3D without a significant price reduction
summary: Analyses show that AMD's new processor offers lower performance than its bigger brother at a similar price, making it less attractive.

title: OpenAI launches Codex Micro, a macropod keyboard with joystick to control AI coding agents
summary: OpenAI's first physical device features 13 backlit keys and a joystick, designed for programmers using artificial intelligence assistants.

title: OpenAI uses AI to automate security testing of its models with GPT-Red
summary: The company develops an automatic 'red teaming' system that finds vulnerabilities in GPT-5.6 Sol more efficiently than humans.

title: Microsoft publishes a record number of security patches, fixing 570 vulnerabilities
summary: The monthly update includes fixes for multiple critical flaws, some of which block updates on certain Dell models.

title: China approves Apple Intelligence with integration of Alibaba and Baidu models
summary: Apple's artificial intelligence receives permission to operate in China, using local providers to meet regulatory requirements.

title: SpaceX shares plummet to IPO price amid uncertainty ahead of Starship launch
summary: Elon Musk's company value falls below its IPO price, wiping out all accumulated gains.

title: Investigation reveals Suno allegedly used YouTube videos to train its AI music generator
summary: The generative music company faces accusations of using copyrighted content without authorization.

title: xAI publishes Grok-Build source code on GitHub after a data leak
summary: Elon Musk's artificial intelligence company releases its model building tool as open source after a security incident.

title: Spotify develops an AI chatbot to answer questions about music and podcasts
summary: The streaming platform tests a conversational assistant that allows users to get information about songs and recommendations.

title: Spotify expands managed accounts for children to free users in several countries
summary: The music service now allows parents to create supervised profiles for their children without needing a Premium subscription, expanding the feature to new regions.

title: CXMT's DDR5 memory shows lower performance than SK hynix in early tests
summary: Analyses of the Chinese chips reveal voltage scaling and overclocking issues, positioning them behind market leaders.

title: RPCS3 emulator manages to run 75% of PS3 games on PC
summary: The PlayStation 3 emulation project reaches a new milestone, approaching full compatibility with the console's library.

title: Windows 11 Steam Machines offer similar performance to SteamOS according to early reviews
summary: Benchmarks show that Steam Machine compatibility with Windows 11 does not sacrifice performance, matching Valve's native operating system.

title: TSMC announces additional $100 billion investment in Arizona to manufacture 2nm chips
summary: The Taiwanese manufacturer expands its presence in the United States with new factories, raising its planned capital expenditure for 2026 to $64 billion.

title: Stripe and Advent International allegedly offered $53.4 billion to acquire PayPal
summary: According to close sources, the joint offer for the payments platform could be one of the largest deals in the fintech sector.

title: Google will allow installation of rival app stores on Android starting July
summary: The company yields to regulatory pressures and opens its ecosystem to alternative marketplaces, in a move that seeks to foster competition.

title: NTSB confirms that the driver of the crashed Tesla in Texas pressed the accelerator fully
summary: The official investigation rules out mechanical or autopilot failures, determining that the driver pressed the pedal to the floor.

title: OpenAI plans to launch a smart speaker with ChatGPT by 2027
summary: The artificial intelligence company would develop an audio-only device with a conversational assistant, without a screen, similar to current smart speakers.
